data.title: Americas Gold and Silver to buy Idaho mine owner Crescent Silver in $65M deal
data.description:
Americas Gold and Silver (USAS) said Thursday it agreed to acquire Crescent Silver, owner of the Crescent silver and antimony mine in Idaho's Silver Valley, for US$65 million in cash and shares.

The acquisition includes $20 million in cash and ~11.1 million Americas Gold and Silver (USAS) common shares valued at ~$45 million based on a price of $4/share; to fund the cash portion and provide additional capital to advance the Crescent Silver mine, the company entered into a bought deal private placement to raise $65 million in gross proceeds.

Crescent is a fully permitted past-producing mine with a historical resource of 3.8 million oz of silver in the measured and indicated category and 19.1 million oz inferred; the mine could produce 1.4 million-1.6 million oz/year of silver, based on a 2015 preliminary economic assessment.

Crescent operated during 1917-81 and includes existing infrastructure such as power, access roads and processing facilities.

data.title: Bioceres targets 40%+ gross margin and $10M–$12M SG&A reduction as it navigates debt dispute and margin gains
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bioceres Crop Solutions Corp. (BIOX) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Federico Trucco highlighted, "Despite the drop in revenues in the quarter compared to the year ago numbers, gross profit remained almost equal at $36 million with a gross margin expansion of 650 basis points." He attributed this to the transition in the seed business model and reduced emphasis on lower-margin, working-capital-intensive sales. Trucco also emphasized, "We have seen a sequential improvement in working capital despite the first quarter's seasonally high needs."
  • Trucco noted, "We committed to operating above the 40% gross margin level, getting closer to 4 months of working capital in terms of annual sales and targeting profitability above 20% of adjusted EBITDA over sales, for which we needed not only to expand margins as we are doing, but also to reduce costs, targeting a $10 million to $12 million reduction in annual SG&A."
  • Trucco added, "Both variable and fixed SG&A have improved significantly, achieving 50% of our top of the range expected annualized savings in just 1 quarter."
  • Head of Investor Relations Paula Savanti reported, "Total revenues for the quarter were $77.5 million, a 17% decline versus the same period last year. The decline reflects to a large degree, the strategy communicated in previous quarters, transitioning our seed business toward a more scalable and capital-efficient model and deprioritizing lower-margin working capital-intensive sales."

Outlook

  • Trucco reiterated the company's commitment to operating above a 40% gross margin and targeting adjusted EBITDA above 20% of sales. He stated, "We have operated well above the gross margin limit we established for ourselves, doubled our percent adjusted EBITDA compared to that of fiscal year '25 and already got to the same level, 17% that we achieved in fiscal '24."
  • Trucco described ongoing cost reduction efforts: "Both variable and fixed SG&A have improved significantly, achieving 50% of our top of the range expected annualized savings in just 1 quarter."
  • Guidance remains focused on further cost cuts and margin expansion, with a continued transition of the seed business and expectation that the revenue decline in seeds will persist for at least two more quarters due to inventory sell-off comparisons.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues were $77.5 million, down 17% year-over-year. Crop Protection revenues were $39.9 million, Crop Nutrition $25.1 million, and Seed and Integrated Products $12.6 million.
  • Gross profit was $36.2 million, down 3% year-over-year, with gross margin rising to 47% from 40% in the year-ago quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $13.6 million, a 61% increase compared to $8.5 million in the same period last year, driven by a $5.9 million reduction in operating costs.
  • Financial debt as of September 30, 2025, stood at $242.5 million, down from $260.2 million at the end of the previous quarter. Net financial debt was $225.9 million, essentially flat quarter-over-quarter.
  • Trucco disclosed, "As we have disclosed in our 6-K filings of October 2 and yesterday, we are undergoing a dispute with holders of our secured convertible and nonconvertible notes. As a result, we've decided to show the noncurrent portion of that debt as current as well as include the prepayment fees that would be owed under an acceleration event."

Q&A

  • Austin Moeller, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about the potential for agricultural imports from Argentina to the U.S. and its impact. Trucco: "In terms of ag inputs, I think also that Argentina being classified on the low tariff end, if you will, on the current trade situation is a benefit to us when we are trying to serve that market from Argentine-manufactured ag input products. Remember that we also hold manufacturing capacity in the U.S. So we are manufacturing most of our bioprotection solutions in-country in the U.S."
  • Moeller: Inquired about Corteva biopesticide sales into Europe. Trucco: "We currently don't have bioprotection products registered in Europe. What we do have is the biostimulant package where Corteva is our main customer in Europe...we have only achieved some marginal sales of biostimulants in Europe in the current quarter. I think most of the biostimulant improvement has been in Argentina and Latin America."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral, focusing on clarification of cross-border opportunities and product registration, without strong skepticism or optimism.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing margin expansion and cost discipline, and remained measured and informative during Q&A. Trucco addressed questions directly and provided context for the company’s operational strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from defensive and reflective on a "very disappointing final quarter" to a more constructive and forward-looking stance, supported by tangible margin and cost improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The previous quarter featured a defensive tone, with management describing the year as the "first down year in the series" and highlighting macroeconomic challenges in Argentina and a disappointing quarter.
  • This quarter, management focused on margin expansion and cost reduction progress, with gross margins improving from 34% to 47% and adjusted EBITDA turning positive.
  • Strategic focus has shifted from managing crisis and stabilizing business to actively executing on cost controls, quality of revenues, and capital efficiency.
  • Analysts’ focus moved from structural questions about cost savings and market risk mitigation to operational execution and product demand drivers, indicating a stabilization of core concerns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Trucco revealed an ongoing dispute with holders of secured convertible and nonconvertible notes, stating, "we are undergoing a dispute with holders of our secured convertible and nonconvertible notes...The company disputes the allegation made by our noteholders and intends to vigorously defend its position."
  • Management acknowledged continued exposure to complex macroeconomic environments, particularly in Argentina, and noted that normalization in key markets remains uneven.
  • Trucco emphasized, "We continue to actively manage liquidity and debt maturities, maintaining constructive dialogue with lenders and prioritizing financial flexibility and disciplined capital allocation."

Final Takeaway

Bioceres Crop Solutions reported a quarter marked by strong margin expansion, significant cost reductions, and steady progress toward its stated profitability and capital efficiency targets. While revenues declined as planned due to the ongoing transition of the seed business, management reiterated its commitment to operating above a 40% gross margin and achieving $10 million to $12 million in annual SG&A savings. The company remains focused on managing liquidity and navigating ongoing debt disputes while emphasizing operational discipline and improved business fundamentals to support long-term resilience and growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stran targets margin expansion and sustained profitability following 56.7% sales growth in Q3 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stran & Company, Inc. (SWAG) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Andrew Shape highlighted the company's transformation from a small startup into a national platform serving over 30 Fortune 500 companies, emphasizing "the same leadership team that built Stran continues to guide us today with that entrepreneurial spirit, and I'm excited for the future of Stran." Sales increased 29% year-over-year to $26 million in Q3 and $87.3 million for the first 9 months of 2025, a 56.7% increase from the same period last year. Shape stated, "Year-to-date, our EBITDA improved by approximately $2.8 million compared to the same period last year, a clear indicator that our strategy to scale responsibly while managing expenses is delivering results." He also noted a successful integration of the Gander Group acquisition and identified strategic M&A as a "key pillar of our growth plan" with a disciplined roll-up strategy.

  • Shape noted the share repurchase program, stating, "We continued our share repurchase program during the third quarter, buying back approximately 267,000 shares of common stock at prices between $1.45 and $1.81 per share, totaling about $408,000. With no debt, $11.8 million in cash and investments, we remain well balanced to fund growth initiatives, pursue acquisitions and continue opportunistic buybacks."

  • CFO David Browner reported, "Sales increased 29% to approximately $26 million for the 3 months ended September 30, 2025, from approximately $20.1 million for the 3 months ended September 30, 2024." He added, "Gross profit increased 18.8% to approximately $7.1 million or 27.2% of sales for the 3 months ended September 30, 2025, from approximately $6 million or 29.5% of sales for the 3 months ended September 30, 2024. Gross profit margin decreased to 27.2%... primarily due to the acquisition of the Gander Group business... which operates at a lower gross margin than the Stran segment."

Outlook

  • Shape stated, "Looking forward, we believe Stran is entering its next phase of maturity, scaling our operations while delivering steady profitability. We see clear path to long-term margin improvement driven by continued operational leverage, technology investments and disciplined execution."

  • The company did not provide explicit financial guidance but emphasized priorities: deepening client relationships, increasing operational efficiency, and maintaining financial discipline.

Financial Results

  • Sales reached $26 million for the quarter, with the Stran segment contributing $17.6 million and the SLS segment (including Gander Group) contributing $8.3 million. Gross profit for the quarter was $7.1 million, with a margin of 27.2%. Operating expenses were $8.9 million, representing 34.1% of sales. Net loss for the quarter was $1.2 million. Cash, cash equivalents, and investments totaled $11.8 million at quarter-end.

  • For the 9 months ended September 30, 2025, sales were $87.3 million. Operating expenses for the 9 months were $27.3 million, and net loss was $1 million. Share repurchases during the quarter totaled approximately 267,000 shares for about $408,000.

Q&A

  • Greg Womack: "How are tariffs count from an accounting perspective? Does that pass on to adding more revenue?" Shape: "When the tariffs happened... it did affect us for some of our orders... we had the opportunity to try to go to some of our customers and ask them if they could pay more. Some of them were agreeable to it, some were not... our cost increase at a greater pace than we were able to charge more. So an analysis... showing direct impact is a 7-figure amount, just over $1 million for direct costs that we weren't able to pass on to our customers."

  • Greg Womack: "Do you guys feel like you're still going to be positive net income for Q4? Or how are you feeling about year-round cash flow positivity?" Shape: "Historically, Q4 has always been our strongest quarter... we're concentrating on continued growth while keeping an eye on managing expenses. So yes, that's our plan. Our plan is to have sustained profitability moving forward, which includes Q4."

  • Vlad [indiscernible]: "How should we think about potential contraction in the economy? How does the business typically perform during contractions?" Shape: "A lot of the business that we have isn't necessarily discretionary... we're spread around multiple verticals... we try to intentionally be diversified in our client base so that we can address any macroeconomic trends... the strength of our balance sheet provides us also with a competitive edge... If the economy does falter, it gives us opportunity to look at additional potential for acquisitions."

  • Vlad [indiscernible]: "What is the methodology that you use to find acquisition targets?" Shape: "We get a lot of inbound inquiries... I attend quite a bit of industry events... there's a lot of people now that within our industry that don't necessarily have a succession plan for their business, and that's where we come in and help them plan... we're being a little bit more scrutinizing of our acquisitions moving forward."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on tariffs, profitability, economic contraction, and acquisitions. Congratulatory remarks were balanced by questions on margin pressure and profit outlook.

  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone during prepared remarks and the Q&A, acknowledging challenges but expressing optimism: "We're concentrating on continued growth while keeping an eye on managing expenses. So yes, that's our plan. Our plan is to have sustained profitability moving forward."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from highly positive to more cautious, reflecting concerns over margin pressure and economic conditions. Management's tone remained confident but more focused on cost control and profitability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 saw slower sequential sales growth compared to Q2 but continued strong year-over-year momentum. Gross margin compressed further due to Gander Group integration and tariff costs, while operating expenses rose at a slower rate than revenue.

  • The previous quarter included new board appointments and highlighted compliance with NASDAQ listing requirements, which was not a focus this quarter.

  • Analyst questions shifted from celebrating growth and buybacks in Q2 to margin, tariffs, profitability, and macro risks in Q3.

  • Management's confidence in scaling, margin expansion, and profitability was consistent, but the emphasis moved more toward operational discipline and cost management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited elevated tariffs leading to increased product costs, buyer hesitation, and margin compression as key risks. Shape acknowledged, "An analysis that we've done showing direct impact is a 7-figure amount, just over $1 million for direct costs that we weren't able to pass on to our customers."

  • Diversification across verticals and a strong balance sheet were presented as mitigants to macroeconomic downturns. Management noted a more scrutinizing approach to future acquisitions.

Final Takeaway

Stran & Company enters the final quarter of 2025 focused on scaling operations, driving margin expansion, and maintaining disciplined growth. Management emphasized strategic M&A, operational efficiencies, and a strong balance sheet as cornerstones for sustainable profitability and shareholder value creation, while remaining optimistic about navigating cost pressures and macroeconomic uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
